description_long: This is a compact, submachine gun-style rifle with a boxy receiver and a prominent, perforated barrel shroud. The weapon features a detachable box magazine inserted vertically in front of the trigger guard, and a pistol grip with a squared-off design. The upper receiver is flat with simple iron sights mounted at the front and rear, and the barrel is relatively short with a series of circular vents along its length, likely for cooling. The finish is matte, with a dark, utilitarian color scheme, and the overall construction appears to be primarily metal with some polymer components. There is no visible buttstock, giving the weapon a compact, easily maneuverable profile suitable for close-quarters urban environments.
description: A compact, boxy submachine gun-style rifle with a perforated barrel shroud, detachable box magazine, and iron sights. The weapon has a matte, dark finish and lacks a visible stock.
description_view_0: The rifle is seen from the muzzle end, showing the circular barrel shroud and the slim profile of the weapon.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the boxy receiver, pistol grip, detachable magazine, iron sights, and perforated barrel shroud.
description_view_2: Viewed from the rear, the rifle's flat upper receiver and alignment of the iron sights are visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the magazine, grip, and barrel shroud from the other side.
